items.title: Massive fire breaks out at popular tourist resort in Dominican Republic

items.content: The reported attacks came less than a day after diplomats briefed on the issue told CBS News Israel and Hezbollah had agreed to a ceasefire.

items.title: Israel continues Lebanon strikes despite fresh ceasefire
items.standfirst: Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon on Saturday morning killed at least 16 people, including two children, according to Lebanese civil defense and media, one day after the U.S. said Israel and Hezbollah had implemented a fresh ceasefire at President Donald Trump’s request
items.content: Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon on Saturday morning killed at least 16 people, including two children, according to Lebanese civil defense and media, one day after the U.S. said Israel and Hezbollah had implemented a fresh ceasefire at President Donald Trump’s request

items.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 16 in southern Lebanon despite reports of renewed ceasefire
items.standfirst: Outbreak of fighting between Hezbollah and Israel has forced mediators to cancel US-Iran peace talks in SwitzerlandIsraeli strikes on southern Lebanon have killed at least 16 people, local authorities said on Saturday, despite reports of a renewed ceasefire aiming to end persistent violence that threatens the new agreement between the US and Iran.Lebanon’s civil defence agency said its personnel transported “16 dead and 12 wounded” to hospital, adding that they had been working “since the early morning hours” in the Nabatieh district in response to “ongoing attacks targeting the area”. Continue reading...
items.content: Outbreak of fighting between Hezbollah and Israel has forced mediators to cancel US-Iran peace talks in SwitzerlandIsraeli strikes on southern Lebanon have killed at least 16 people, local authorities said on Saturday, despite reports of a renewed ceasefire aiming to end persistent violence that threatens the new agreement between the US and Iran.Lebanon’s civil defence agency said its personnel transported “16 dead and 12 wounded” to hospital, adding that they had been working “since the early morning hours” in the Nabatieh district in response to “ongoing attacks targeting the area”. Continue reading...

items.title: Could Israel lose Donald Trump’s support by continuing to drop bombs in Lebanon?
items.standfirst: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed at least seven people, including two children, hours after reports emerged of a ceasefire agreement. The persistent fighting threatened an interim agreement between the United States and Iran to end the war in the Middle East. Could Israel' Netanyahu lose Trump's support if strikes continue? Analysis by Yossi Mekelberg, senior consulting fellow at MENAP, Chatam House, London.
items.content: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed at least seven people, including two children, hours after reports emerged of a ceasefire agreement. The persistent fighting threatened an interim agreement between the United States and Iran to end the war in the Middle East. Could Israel' Netanyahu lose Trump's support if strikes continue? Analysis by Yossi Mekelberg, senior consulting fellow at MENAP, Chatam House, London.

items.content: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed at least seven people, including two children, hours after reports emerged of a ceasefire agreement. (Image credit: Leo Correa)

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement

items.content: Several people are reported killed in southern Lebanon, with the IDF saying it hit "Hezbollah terrorist targets" after the group fired over 50 projectiles at Israeli forces.

items.title: Residents flee as Israeli strikes target southern Lebanon
items.standfirst: Lebanese official media said fresh Israeli strikes on the country's south on Saturday killed five people, despite a new ceasefire between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah group announced a day earlier.
items.content: Lebanese official media said fresh Israeli strikes on the country's south on Saturday killed five people, despite a new ceasefire between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah group announced a day earlier.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah renew ceasefire after deadly flareup disrupts opening of Iran talks
items.standfirst: JD Vance pulls out of US-Iran meeting in Switzerland on implementation of peace deal after clashes in southern LebanonMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael and Hezbollah agreed to renew a fragile ceasefire in Lebanon on Friday after 24 hours of intense violence that posed an early challenge to the new agreement between the US and Iran to end their conflict.A meeting that was scheduled to take place on Friday between Washington and Tehran in Switzerland to discuss implementation of the new deal was cancelled when Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 47 people. Continue reading...
items.content: JD Vance pulls out of US-Iran meeting in Switzerland on implementation of peace deal after clashes in southern LebanonMiddle East crisis – live updatesIsrael and Hezbollah agreed to renew a fragile ceasefire in Lebanon on Friday after 24 hours of intense violence that posed an early challenge to the new agreement between the US and Iran to end their conflict.A meeting that was scheduled to take place on Friday between Washington and Tehran in Switzerland to discuss implementation of the new deal was cancelled when Hezbollah killed four Israeli soldiers and Israel carried out a wave of retaliatory airstrikes in southern Lebanon and the Bekaa valley that killed at least 47 people. Continue reading...

items.title: Middle East live: At least 16 killed in southern Lebanon as Israel, Hezbollah trade blame
items.standfirst: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed at least 16 people despite a ceasefire agreed with Hezbollah only a day earlier. Israel and Hezbollah accused each other of violating the truce, with the Israeli military alleging breaches by the Iran-backed group and Hezbollah saying it had the right to respond to attacks. The violence unfolded as US envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi headed to Switzerland for talks. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday killed at least 16 people despite a ceasefire agreed with Hezbollah only a day earlier. Israel and Hezbollah accused each other of violating the truce, with the Israeli military alleging breaches by the Iran-backed group and Hezbollah saying it had the right to respond to attacks. The violence unfolded as US envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi headed to Switzerland for talks. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Details on Israel and Hezbollah's ceasefire amid delayed U.S.-Iran talks
items.standfirst: Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon as officials say fighting between them has delayed U.S.-Iran peace talks. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab and Margaret Brennan report.
items.content: Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon as officials say fighting between them has delayed U.S.-Iran peace talks. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab and Margaret Brennan report.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire after intensified fighting threatens U.S.-Iran talks
items.standfirst: Israel and militant group Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire Friday, after a deadly escalation between the two in Lebanon looked set to derail Washington-Tehran peace talks in Switzerland
items.content: Israel and militant group Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire Friday, after a deadly escalation between the two in Lebanon looked set to derail Washington-Tehran peace talks in Switzerland

clusters.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One converted from luxury jet gifted by Qatar
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump unveils new Air Force One, a converted Qatari 747
bullet_summary: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet
Reported by France24, Sky News World, CBS News and others

items.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One converted from luxury jet gifted by Qatar
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Friday unveiled a new Air Force One converted from a former Qatari-owned Boeing 747, calling it a "flying White House" while touting the aircraft's luxury interior. The plane will serve as a temporary replacement as presidential jets ordered directly from Boeing have been delayed until 2028.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Friday unveiled a new Air Force One converted from a former Qatari-owned Boeing 747, calling it a "flying White House" while touting the aircraft's luxury interior. The plane will serve as a temporary replacement as presidential jets ordered directly from Boeing have been delayed until 2028.

items.title: Trump unveils new converted Air Force One jet he accepted as gift from Qatar
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has unveiled the new Air Force One, an upgraded Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar that has been converted into the official US presidential aircraft.
items.content: Donald Trump has unveiled the new Air Force One, an upgraded Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar that has been converted into the official US presidential aircraft.

items.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, a $400 million plane gifted by Qatar
items.standfirst: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.
items.content: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.

items.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, gifted from Qatar
items.standfirst: President Trump unveiled what's meant to become the new Air Force One, a gift from the government of Qatar. Plus, new fighting erupted between Israel and Lebanon before they signed a new ceasefire. Nicole Killion has all this and more from the White House.
items.content: President Trump unveiled what's meant to become the new Air Force One, a gift from the government of Qatar. Plus, new fighting erupted between Israel and Lebanon before they signed a new ceasefire. Nicole Killion has all this and more from the White House.

items.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, a converted Qatari 747
items.standfirst: New craft, called VC-25B Bridge, had provoked protest since $400m jet wildly exceeds limit on unsolicited giftsDonald Trump unveiled the new, temporary Air Force One at a hangar at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland on Friday, a day after White House officials bid farewell to one of two Boeing 747s used to transport presidents for more than 30 years.The new jet, designated VC-25B and decked out in a punchy red, white, dark blue and gold livery, was gifted to Trump by the Gulf emirate of Qatar, provoking howls of political protest since the $400m jet wildly exceeds the limit on unsolicited gifts of $50 in value in a single calendar year from the same source. Continue reading...
items.content: New craft, called VC-25B Bridge, had provoked protest since $400m jet wildly exceeds limit on unsolicited giftsDonald Trump unveiled the new, temporary Air Force One at a hangar at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland on Friday, a day after White House officials bid farewell to one of two Boeing 747s used to transport presidents for more than 30 years.The new jet, designated VC-25B and decked out in a punchy red, white, dark blue and gold livery, was gifted to Trump by the Gulf emirate of Qatar, provoking howls of political protest since the $400m jet wildly exceeds the limit on unsolicited gifts of $50 in value in a single calendar year from the same source. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump hits back at Meloni after she said he 'completely made up' claim she 'begged' him for picture
clusters.neutral_headline: Italy's Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo at G7
bullet_summary: After President Donald Trump said Giorgia Meloni had “begged” him for a photo at the G7 summit earlier this week, Italy’s Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was quick to hit back
Reported by Sky News World, France24, NBC World and others

items.title: Trump hits back at Meloni after she said he 'completely made up' claim she 'begged' him for picture
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has hit back at Italian prime minister Giorgia Meloni after she accused him of making up a story that she begged him for a picture.
items.content: Donald Trump has hit back at Italian prime minister Giorgia Meloni after she accused him of making up a story that she begged him for a picture.

items.title: 'Neither I nor Italy ever beg': Meloni hits back at Trump claim that she begged for a photo
items.standfirst: Italy and the United States have plunged into a diplomatic spat on Friday, after Donald Trump told Italian media that its prime minister had "begged him for a photo" at the recent G7 summit. It immediately sparked outrage in Italian political circles and beyond, drawing a response from Georgia Meloni herself as the fallout continues. Story by Luke Shrago.
items.content: Italy and the United States have plunged into a diplomatic spat on Friday, after Donald Trump told Italian media that its prime minister had "begged him for a photo" at the recent G7 summit. It immediately sparked outrage in Italian political circles and beyond, drawing a response from Georgia Meloni herself as the fallout continues. Story by Luke Shrago.

items.title: Top Italian diplomat cancels U.S. trip after prime minister slams Trump's claim about G7 photo
items.standfirst: Italy's top diplomat canceled a trip to the U.S. after Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni slammed President Trump's claims that she "begged" him for a photo at the G7 summit. The dispute erupted after Trump told Italian broadcaster La7 that Meloni had pleaded with him for a photo at the meeting of leading industrialized nations in the French resort of Evian-les-Bains earlier this week.
items.content: Italy's top diplomat canceled a trip to the U.S. after Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni slammed President Trump's claims that she "begged" him for a photo at the G7 summit. The dispute erupted after Trump told Italian broadcaster La7 that Meloni had pleaded with him for a photo at the meeting of leading industrialized nations in the French resort of Evian-les-Bains earlier this week.

items.title: Italy's Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo at G7
items.standfirst: The highly public exchange is an indication that their earlier close ties have frayed since Trump's decision to go to war with Iran.
items.content: The highly public exchange is an indication that their earlier close ties have frayed since Trump's decision to go to war with Iran.

clusters.title: Opinion: Algae doesn't care about our party lines
clusters.neutral_headline: Opinion: Algae doesn't care about our party lines
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, France24, CBS News

items.title: Opinion: Algae doesn't care about our party lines
items.standfirst: President Trump's beautification project of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has become plagued with a robust algae bloom, despite a $14 million investment and a coating of "American flag blue."
items.content: President Trump's beautification project of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has become plagued with a robust algae bloom, despite a $14 million investment and a coating of "American flag blue."(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)

items.title: Paint from Trump renovation peels off Washington's Reflecting Pool
items.standfirst: The iconic reflecting pool, which stretches between Lincoln Memorial and the Washington Monument on DC's National Mall has turned green due to proliferation of algae in the water. It comes as the pool was recently reopened following renovation work ordered by Donald Trump that saw it painted American flag blue. Less than two weeks later, the paint is peeling off. Story by Camille Corcoran.
items.content: The iconic reflecting pool, which stretches between Lincoln Memorial and the Washington Monument on DC's National Mall has turned green due to proliferation of algae in the water. It comes as the pool was recently reopened following renovation work ordered by Donald Trump that saw it painted American flag blue. Less than two weeks later, the paint is peeling off. Story by Camille Corcoran.

items.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae turn water green
items.standfirst: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.
items.content: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

items.title: Peeled paint appears in Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after algae bloom
items.standfirst: President Trump's multi-million dollar makeover of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is not going swimmingly. CBS News' Mark Strassmann reports.
items.content: President Trump's multi-million dollar makeover of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is not going swimmingly. CBS News' Mark Strassmann reports.

clusters.title: Slave trade reenacted on Juneteenth as leaders demand reparations
clusters.neutral_headline: Slave trade reenacted on Juneteenth as leaders demand reparations
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, Guardian World

items.title: Slave trade reenacted on Juneteenth as leaders demand reparations
items.standfirst: Ghana has been hosting a conference to advance the UN resolution declaring slavery the ‘gravest crime against humanity.'
items.content: Ghana has been hosting a conference to advance the UN resolution declaring slavery the ‘gravest crime against humanity.'

items.title: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark conference in Ghana
items.standfirst: Ensuring fair compensation for those affected by legacies of enslavement and measures to address debt burdens, part of 18-point strategic roadmapMore than money: the logic of slavery reparationsA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana.Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...
items.content: Ensuring fair compensation for those affected by legacies of enslavement and measures to address debt burdens, part of 18-point strategic roadmapMore than money: the logic of slavery reparationsA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana.Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

items.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
items.standfirst: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countriesBarbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement.Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...
items.content: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countriesBarbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement.Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Scientists Invent a Way to Brew Espresso With Ultrasonic Waves—No Hot Water Required
clusters.neutral_headline: Sound waves made this espresso—and could cut brewing energy use by 75%
bullet_summary: Reported by Wired, Reuters Top News

items.title: Scientists Invent a Way to Brew Espresso With Ultrasonic Waves—No Hot Water Required
items.standfirst: Researchers have demonstrated they can make coffee comparable to conventional espresso using ultrasonic waves. Because the process doesn’t need hot water, it consumes 75 percent less energy.
items.content: Researchers have demonstrated they can make coffee comparable to conventional espresso using ultrasonic waves. Because the process doesn’t need hot water, it consumes 75 percent less energy.

items.title: Sound waves made this espresso—and could cut brewing energy use by 75%
items.standfirst: Ultrasonic espresso uses cold water and high-frequency sound waves to extract coffee with far less energy than conventional brewing. Early taste tests suggest it can match the traditional version. [theconversation.com]
items.content: Ultrasonic espresso uses cold water and high-frequency sound waves to extract coffee with far less energy than conventional brewing. Early taste tests suggest it can match the traditional version. [theconversation.com]

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, CBS News

items.title: James Burrows, legendary director of Cheers and Friends, dies aged 85
items.standfirst: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.
items.content: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.

items.title: "Cheers" co-creator James Burrows dies at 85
items.standfirst: Director James Burrows co-created the landmark comedy "Cheers" and directed more than 50 pilot episodes. He died at 85 on Friday.
items.content: Director James Burrows co-created the landmark comedy "Cheers" and directed more than 50 pilot episodes. He died at 85 on Friday.
